text: Setting clear goals at the start of a project is essential for success. A useful approach is to set SMART goals, which are Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. For example, instead of saying “improve the website,” a SMART goal would be “increase page load speed by 20% within three months.” Next, break the project into smaller milestones, such as completing the design phase or testing a prototype. This helps track progress and keeps the team motivated. You should also define key deliverables—tangible results like reports or software updates—for each milestone. Finally, stakeholder alignment is crucial. Make sure everyone involved agrees on the goals and expectations to avoid confusion later.
